title: Tanzania Food, Drugs and Cosmetics (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Food) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Tanzania Food, Drugs and Cosmetics (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Food) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Tanzania Food, Drugs and Cosmetics (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Food) Regulations, 2006 and shall come into operation on the date of publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all areas in which the Act applies and shall affect all foods which are unfit for human consumption, or suspected of being unfit for human consumption or any food which is condemned by a Court under section 34(4) of the Act , or which is in any other way, found by an inspector to be manufactured, imported, distributed, sold, offered or exposed for sale, contrary to any of the provisions of the Act or these Regulations.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Tanzania Food Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 2003; " documentary food sample " means a sample obtained by copying the documents covering the sale of food in question without collecting an actual food sample where such food sample would be too heavy, expensive, bulky or one not possible of collection; " formal or official food sample " means a food collected and divided in accordance w ith section 103 of the Act ; “ informal or unofficial food sample ” means a food sample collected for the purpose of obtaining information and need not necessarily be analysed; " post seizure food sample " means a sample collected by an order of a court in respect of food under seizure; " sampling form " means a form for the collection of a sample as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations; " seizure certificate " means a certificate set out in the Schedule to these Regulations issued by inspector in respect of seized food which is unfit for human consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for human consumption; " unfit food " means a food which violates any provision of the Act or any regulations made under these Regulations; " voluntary condemnation certificate " means a certificate issued by Inspector in respect of seized food which is unfit for human consumption; " voluntary surrender " means the wilful act of handing over to inspector a food which is unfit or suspected of being unfit for human consumption.

provisions.title: Voluntary surrender
provisions.content: (1) A food owner or his agent may, on being satisfied that his food voluntary is unfit for sale for human consumption, voluntarily surrender it to inspector for condemnation. (2) The Inspector shall, prepare for the owner or his agent a voluntary surrender or condemnation certificate in the form set out in the Schedule to these Regulations in respect of that food if he is so satisfied that food is unfit for sale for human consumption and that food shall be destroyed or disposed of in a similar manner as condemned by order of a court.

provisions.title: Reconditioning of food
provisions.content: (1) Any food which is in violation of the Act or Regulations made thereunder may at the discretions of an Inspector and with the agreement of the owner of the food or his agent, be re-conditioned in order to make it fit for human consumption, or for removal of any misrepresantation or for diverting from sale for human consumption to any other use such as for sale for animal feed, as the case may be. (2) An inspector yvho decides to re-condition violative food shall be responsible for seeing that the process he has chosen is complete and effective and shall also get a representative sample yyhen necessary to check the acceptability of the final product and account for the entire lot to guard against manipulation of the lot or part of it by owner or his agent.

provisions.title: Seizure of food
provisions.content: Where an Inspector seizes food which is unfit for human Seizure of consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for human consumption he may— (a) mark it and remove the same from where he found it and inform the owner or his agent, of his intention to have it dealt with by a Court; or (b) mark it in accordance with section 99 (2)(a) of the Act and leave it under the care of the owner or his agent and in either case an Inspector who seizes such food shall issue an appropriate seizure certificate set out in the Schedule to these Regulations in respect of that food.

provisions.title: Procedure for disposal
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector who seizes food which is unfit for sale for human dispos it consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for sale for human consumption in accordance with the Act shall— (a) inform the person owning the food, or his agent, of the exact reasons of unfitness of such food; (b) where the unfitness is such that the food cannot be re­conditioned to make it fit for sale for human consumption, the inspector shall further inform the owner of the food, or his agent, of the formof disposal he intends to use in destroying or disposing of the food. (2) The owner of food so seized, or his agent, may agree or disagee with the view's of an Inspector in respect of the unfitness of his food so seized and w here such owner, or his agent, agree with the views of an Inspector, the Inspector shall ask the owner or his agent to signify his agreement to the effect by signing a voluntary surrender or condemnation certificate in respect of that food, as the case may be. (3) Where the views of an Inspector are rejected by the owner of seized food, or his agent, he shall take the food to the Court whose decision on the condition of the food shall be binding on both parties. (4) If the Court confirms the views of the Inspector in respect of food taken before it, the Inspector shall have the duty to: (a) dispose the food in accordance with the decision of the Court and make sure that the method of disposal used completely removes the food from the channels of commerce of all kinds by rendering it impossible of being eaten by the sane person; and (b) prepare a compulsory condemnation certificate in the Form prescribed in the Schedule to these Regulations in respect of that food and give it to the owner or his agent. (5) In addition to any punitive measure, ordered by the court, the owner of the food in question shall bear the cost of disposing of such food.

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The Food (Control of Quality) (Treatment and Disposal of Unit Food) Regulations of 1994 are hereby revoked. [GN. No 82 of 1994] Schedule (Made under regulation 3 ) Form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. definitions.definition: the Tanzania Food Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 2003

a sample obtained by copying the documents covering the sale of food in question without collecting an actual food sample where such food sample would be too heavy, expensive, bulky or one not possible of collection
a food collected and divided in accordance w ith section 103 of the Act
a food sample collected for the purpose of obtaining information and need not necessarily be analysed
a sample collected by an order of a court in respect of food under seizure
a form for the collection of a sample as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations

a certificate set out in the Schedule to these Regulations issued by inspector in respect of seized food which is unfit for human consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for human consumption
a food which violates any provision of the Act or any regulations made under these Regulations
a certificate issued by Inspector in respect of seized food which is unfit for human consumption
the wilful act of handing over to inspector a food which is unfit or suspected of being unfit for human consumption.
